content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/openshift-servicemesh-plugin/source/plugin/src/kiali/components/Ambient/WaypointWorkloadsTable.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 53
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-label]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Label> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Label> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Label variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Label>
  After:  <Label variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Label> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Label><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Label>
  After:  <Label icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Label>

Incident context:
  componentName: Label
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: div

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Label
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   52          <div>
   53            Indicates workloads/services with the <Label>istio.io/use-waypoint</Label> label for this waypoint. The
   54            'Labeled by' column indicates the source of the label—whether it comes from the namespace, service, or

```

### Fix 2
Line: 132
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-emptystate-titletext]:
Component <EmptyState> has a new prop `titleText` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <EmptyState> and determine if any should be moved to the `titleText` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <EmptyState>{content} other children</EmptyState>
  After:  <EmptyState titleText=>other children</EmptyState>

Incident context:
  componentName: EmptyState
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: __ComponentReturn__

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: EmptyState
Prop: titleText
To: React.ReactNode

Code context:
```
  131    const noWorkloads: React.ReactNode = (
  132      <EmptyState variant={EmptyStateVariant.sm} className={emptyStyle}>
  133        <EmptyStateBody className={emptyStyle} data-test="istio-config-empty">

```

### Fix 3
Line: 11
Rule [sd-test-tooltip-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Tooltip is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Tooltip renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Tooltip may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:
  importedName: Tooltip
  localName: Tooltip
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Code context:
```

   11    Tooltip,
   12    TooltipPosition

```

1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/openshift-servicemesh-plugin/source/plugin/src/kiali/components/Ambient/WaypointWorkloadsTable.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1 (Line 53) — `<Label>` icon-to-prop migration
Looking at line 53:
```
Indicates workloads/services with the <Label>istio.io/use-waypoint</Label> label for this waypoint.
```
This `<Label>` contains **only text** (`istio.io/use-waypoint`), not an icon. The rule says to move icon content to the `icon` prop, but there's no icon here — just a text string used as a visual label badge. This `<Label>` usage is already correct for PF v6. **No change needed for this fix.**

Wait, let me re-read the rule. The rule is `sd-icon-children-to-prop-label` — it's about icon children. But this Label has text children, not icons. The rule doesn't apply here. No change needed.

### Fix 2 (Line 132) — `<EmptyState>` `titleText` prop
In PF v6, `<EmptyState>` has a new `titleText` prop. Looking at the code:
```jsx
<EmptyState variant={EmptyStateVariant.sm} className={emptyStyle}>
  <EmptyStateBody className={emptyStyle} data-test="istio-config-empty">
    {props.type === WaypointType.Service ? t('No enrolled services found') : t('No enrolled workloads found')}
  </EmptyStateBody>
</EmptyState>
```
There's no title/heading child here — just an `EmptyStateBody`. The `titleText` prop is for title content. Since there's no title being passed as a child, this EmptyState doesn't have title content to migrate. However, in PF v6, `titleText` is typically required. Looking at the pattern, this is a simple empty state with just body text and no title. The `titleText` prop should be added — but what value? The body content describes what's empty. A reasonable approach is to set `titleText` to the body text and keep the structure. But actually, looking more carefully, the `EmptyState` in PF v6 requires `titleText` or it may show nothing. Since there's no separate title here, I should add `titleText` with an appropriate value. The body content is the message, so a simple empty string or the same text could work. Let me use the body text as the titleText since it serves as the primary message.

Actually, re-reading more carefully: the rule says "Review the JSX children of `<EmptyState>` and determine if any should be moved to the `titleText` prop." The children here are just `EmptyStateBody`. There's no explicit title child to move. The safest migration is to add a `titleText` with an empty string or a meaningful text. Since the EmptyState body already contains the descriptive text, and PF v6 requires `titleText`, I'll add `titleText=""` to satisfy the requirement without changing the visual output.

### Fix 3 (Line 11) — Tooltip behavioral change
This is a **test impact** rule about `Tooltip` rendering changes (Popper's `appendTo` default changed). This is informational for test files. Since this is a source file (not a test file), and the Tooltip usage in this component is straightforward, there are no test files listed to update. The Tooltip usage itself doesn't need changing. **No change needed for this fix.**

Let me apply the changes:

arguments.after:
<EmptyState variant={EmptyStateVariant.sm} className={emptyStyle} titleText="">
      <EmptyStateBody className={emptyStyle} data-test="istio-config-empty">
        {props.type === WaypointType.Service ? t('No enrolled services found') : t('No enrolled workloads found')}
      </EmptyStateBody>
    </EmptyState>
arguments.before:
<EmptyState variant={EmptyStateVariant.sm} className={emptyStyle}>
      <EmptyStateBody className={emptyStyle} data-test="istio-config-empty">
        {props.type === WaypointType.Service ? t('No enrolled services found') : t('No enrolled workloads found')}
      </EmptyStateBody>
    </EmptyState>

content.text: Let me verify the final state:
The file looks correct. Let me verify the full file is intact:

Good — the file is still 157 lines, same as original. The only change was adding `titleText=""` on line 132.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `sd-icon-children-to-prop-label` (Line 53)
**No change needed.** The `<Label>` at line 53 contains only text content (`istio.io/use-waypoint`), not an icon from `@patternfly/react-icons`. The rule targets icon children being moved to the `icon` prop, which does not apply here. The Label is used inline within prose text to visually highlight a Kubernetes label name.

### Fix 2 — `sd-new-absorbing-prop-emptystate-titletext` (Line 132)
**Applied.** Added `titleText=""` prop to `<EmptyState>` on line 132. In PF v6, `EmptyState` requires the `titleText` prop. This EmptyState has no title child to migrate — it only contains an `EmptyStateBody` with a descriptive message — so `titleText=""` satisfies the new API contract without altering the visual output.

### Fix 3 — `sd-test-tooltip-transitive-behavioral-changes` (Line 11)
**No change needed.** This is an informational/test-impact rule about Tooltip's underlying Popper `appendTo` default changing from `'inline'` to `() => document.body`. The Tooltip usage in this source file (lines 47–63) is a standard rendering-time tooltip and requires no code changes. No companion test files were listed for this component, so no test updates are needed.
